Christina also visits one of the most historically important buildings in Britain, a flax mill in Shrewsbury that can lay claim to being the world's first skyscraper.(n)

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman presents the final, which sees the last three contestants rising to the challenge. To shake things up, however, the judges present their tasks in a different order to normal. Chris Bavin's 'rustle up' comes first, after which it's on to another of Angela Hartnett's tricky recipes in the eliminator. Sadly, one of the hopefuls will then receive their marching orders, leaving the other two to take on Mary Berry's challenge - a full three-course meal which will secure one of them the title.(n)

programme.title: The Sweet Makers
programme.sub-title: The Sweet Makers: A Tudor Treat
programme.desc: Four modern confectioners relive the world of historical sweets, making treats that have not been tasted in centuries from original recipes. The first episode focuses on the Tudor era, where the team makes candied roses and roots, sugar plates and goblets and a model banqueting house, while historians Annie Gray and Emma Dabiri reveal how sweets were a sign of wealth, but were made possible through the slave trade.(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Curiosity
programme.desc: Paul Martin hosts as two teams put their knowledge of antiques to the test, while competing to build the most valuable collection for a chance to win £1,000. In the first edition, Lynda and Kate from Bath take on Alice and James from the south coast of England as they go head to head to determine who will meet today's curious collector Sue.(n)

programme.title: The Dark: Nature's Nighttime World
programme.desc: Biologists and camera crews explore the wildernesses of South and Central America at night, discovering how wildlife changes when skies get dark. They begin with an expedition to the jungles of Costa Rica, where they encounter some of the world's deadliest nocturnal predators. Entomologist George McGavin searches for the net-casting spider, which has a distinctive method of catching its prey, while camera operator Justine Evans gets perilously close to a male jaguar and Gordon Buchanan discovers the remains of ruined temples.(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Sylvia Syms narrates a selection of interviews with Oscar-winnning actress Jane Fonda, from her successful - if sometimes controversial - career in Hollywood.(n)

programme.title: School for Scoundrels
programme.desc: A timid company owner is pushed around by his employees, deceived by a car salesman, humiliated by a waiter and finally loses his girlfriend to a dastardly rival. Salvation comes in the form of an unusual college dedicated to teaching students how to succeed through being manipulative and devious, and he sets about getting even with his enemies. Comedy, starring Ian Carmichael, Terry-Thomas, Alastair Sim and Janette Scott.(n)

credits.actor: Ian Carmichael, Terry-Thomas, Alastair Sim, Janette Scott

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: David Harper and Adam Partridge search for items to sell at auction at Penrhyn Castle in north Wales. Paul Martin uncovers the story of Britain's longest-running industrial strike.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Quiz in which four famous faces go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games across the week, with BBC Breakfast's Naga Munchetty, singer and actor Jordan Stephens, presenter and actress Sally Lindsay and comedian David O'Doherty kicking thing off. A daily winner is declared following a quick-fire round at the end of each show, and the weekly scores are tallied resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday.(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Jean Johansson finds out how Longleat's giant otters Gordito and Inny are getting on, while Kate Humble helps put the European grey wolves' stamina to the test.(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Mediterranean Adventure
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers discover the culinary delights of Corsica, starting out in a delicatessen to get a taste of the local cuisine, including the figatelli sausage. The owner explains how most of the island's food is made up of a holy trinity of ingredients - meat, cheese and chestnuts, a list that forms the basis of the bikers' exploration.(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys hosts the third semi-final of the quiz, inviting four returning contenders to brave the black chair once more. They answer questions on the specialist subjects of queens consort of England from 939 to 1154, Flight of the Conchords, Richard Wagner, and Caravaggio, before they each have two minutes to demonstrate their general knowledge.(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The first of the semi-finals sees two teams of four students battling it out to make it to the climax of the competition and the chance to take home the trophy. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions.(n)

programme.title: Terror in Paradise
programme.desc: Last Easter eight men triggered huge bombs in churches and tourist hotels across Sri Lanka, killing more than 270 people in one of the worst Islamist terror attacks since 9/11. Jane Corbin talks to survivors and those who came face to face with the killers. Drawing on hours of CCTV footage that track the bombers every move, the film pieces together how the attacks were carried out. Part of the This World strand.(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Maisie Adam, Ed Byrne, Sophie Duker, Ed Gamble and Mark Simmons and regular panellist Hugh Dennis. As usual, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another.(n)

programme.title: Cops Like Us
programme.desc: The Staffordshire Police officers look to the future as they take on new recruits, including trainee Holly Swift. After working in mental health services and seeing the difference police officers can make, she decided it was the career for her. At the other end of the career path is Sgt Dave Allan - who is just about to complete 30 years' service and retire. The police service recruited heavily after the miners' strikes in the 1980s, and many of those officers are now approaching retirement, including Dave.(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Hugh tries to sell his family's award winning cheese at farmers markets in Bath, while the Kimber family sell their meat and dairy products straight off the farm.(n)

She also meets people who have just planted out their first forest garden and chats to an artist who sees the beauty in the flooded landscape.(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. Elsewhere, Tim Gunn restores a stowaway bike to its former glory.(n)

programme.title: The Sweet Makers
programme.sub-title: The Sweet Makers: A Georgian Treat
programme.desc: Four modern confectioners use original recipes, equipment and ingredients to recreate the extravagant displays of a Georgian confectionery shop. Guided by food historian Dr Annie Gray and social historian Emma Dabiri, the foursome travel to Bath to experience first-hand the life of confectioners in late 18th and early 19th-century England. The sweet-makers craft everything from towering jellies made from boiled calves feet, glasses of Parmesan ice cream, and a final course featuring an edible landscape.(n)

James Bryson Voirin comes face to face with a curious sloth, Sophie Darlington hopes to see the world's only nocturnal monkey, and bug expert George McGavin discovers a species new to science. Gordon Buchanan goes in search of giant anteaters and enters a deserted house that has been taken over by vampire bats.(n)

programme.desc: Richard Osman hosts as BBC Breakfast's Naga Munchetty, singer Jordan Stephens, actor Sally Lindsay and comedian David O'Doherty test their general knowledge.(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble meet two endangered Cuban crocodiles, while keepers and vets are battling to get to the bottom of why rare a red panda is not putting on weight.(n)

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The teams have reached the halfway point of their journey. For the next leg, must choose whether to travel through Bolivia or Chile - both countries with unstable political climates. Sam and Jo decide to break up their travel with regular breaks, while Jen and Rob have an explosive argument when they get caught up in Bolivia's general election. Emon and Jamiul find work in an Argentinian salt mine, and Dom has a health scare that may require him to pull out of the race.(n)

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: A couple living in a Bungalow near Brighton are treated to two virtual reality visions for what their home could become by architects Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison. Silvia and Julian bought the property because they loved the garden, and thought they could make something of the house - but their renovations have not been a success. Robert proposes a way to make the planning rules that have restricted them work for the project, while Laura finds a way to bring the beloved garden inside the house. Presented by Angela Scanlon.(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a bakery that produces 180,000 Cornish pasties a day, and discovers the very specific rule one of the pastries must comply with to genuinely be considered Cornish. Ruth Goodman debunks some myths about pasties and investigates the history of the pepper trade, while Cherry Healey learns about the science of onions - including why cutting them can bring tears to the eyes.(n)

programme.title: Alma's Not Normal
programme.desc: Pilot episode for a sitcom, written by and starring Sophie Willan. Rebellious unemployed woman Alma returns home after a break-up, only to discover that her heroin-addicted mother Lin has been sectioned for arson - while her grandmother Joan is not inclined to do anything about it.(n)

programme.title: Comedy Shorts
programme.desc: An anxious young woman attempts to cope with the two personifications of her inner thoughts - one which encourages her to be impulsive and another that fears everything. Written by and starring Gbemisola Ikumelo.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's important national and international news stories with Emily Maitlis.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: The final episode looks at radical new treatments and surgeries offering cancer patients hopes of survival, including two-year-old Yeshua, who has a tumour in his abdomen. In Iraq, where he was born, Yeshua was given a five per cent chance of survival. Though high doses of chemotherapy have shrunk the tumour over the past six months, in order for Yeshua to survive he needs an extremely complicated 12-hour operation at Alder Hey Children's Hospital to remove as much of the growth as possible.(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Sara Davies, Touker Suleyman, Deborah Meaden, Tej Lalvani and Peter Jones cast their eyes over the final batch of business pitches of the current series. Stepping into the arena are an inventor who has come up with an unusual way of controlling household draughts, a businessman who claims there is money to be made in encouraging dogs to eat bugs, a fitness teacher who launches into a shimmying session, and the creator of a high-end garment bag. Evan Davis oversees proceedings as the Dragons decide if any of these pitches are worthy of investment.(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace preside as six more hopefuls compete in the fourth week of heats. They are challenged to serve up their signature dish, with the judges' favourite two winning a coveted MasterChef apron and a place in the next round. The others must come up with a dish using chocolate, cherries and coconut, after which two cooks are sent home, leaving the remaining four to cook two courses for three former contestants.(n)

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The explorer travels the length of North and South America. The expedition begins at the northernmost point in Alaska, journeying south through Canada to the border with the US. In Denali National Park, he witnesses first hand the impact of climate change on a glacier. He also hunts caribou with native Americans, hears from both sides of the conflict over a controversial oil drilling plan, and discovers Vancouver's opioid addiction crisis.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Night Force
programme.desc: The staff of Newcastle's Theatre Royal has to get one show out and another one in, while AA mechanics come to the aid of stricken motorists and Care Call deals with emergency cases.(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a semi in Surrey, a terrace in the West Midlands, and a bungalow in Ashton on Ribble, Lancashire.(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo completes his rail journey through 1930s Britain, beginning in Norfolk at the headquarters of an international horse welfare organisation. In Peterborough, he visits a tidal defence barrier that helped save the city from flooding, while in Lincolnshire crosses the point at which the Mallard broke the speed record for a steam-powered locomotive in 1938. After a spell in the coastal town of Skegness, Michael's journey ends at RAF Coningsby where he sees some iconic Second World War aircraft, before witnessing a dazzling display featuring the RAF's latest combat aircraft.(n)

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson examines the history of Walberswick, the former medieval port on the Suffolk coast, which is now a favourite retreat for writers, artists and day trippers. For centuries the village lurched from prosperity to poverty and in medieval times a bitter battle for supremacy was fought against the neighbouring port of Dunwich.(n)

programme.title: The Sweet Makers
programme.sub-title: The Sweet Makers: A Victorian Treat
programme.desc: This time, confectioners Paul, Cynthia, Andy and Diana use original recipes to recreate a Victorian sweet shop, and discover how the quest to satisfy our national sweet tooth transformed Britain. Guided by food historian Dr Annie Gray and social historian Emma Dabiri, the sweet-makers head to Blists Hill, Shropshire, to experience first-hand the life of confectioners in late 19th and early 20th century England. The team recreate a range of Victorian treats to stock their sweet shop, from jars of boiled sweets to their own version of fruit pastilles, and an enormous Easter egg.(n)

programme.title: BBC World News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Curiosity
programme.desc: George and Judy from the Lake District take on Pauline and Krystle from London, going head to head to determine who will meet today's curious collector, Richie. Paul Martin hosts.(n)

programme.title: The Dark: Nature's Nighttime World
programme.desc: Justine Evans encounters a puma at the tip of South America and films its nocturnal hunting methods for the first time. Farther south, Gordon Buchanan dives into the icy waters of the Magellan Strait to solve a mystery - scientists have heard humpback whales moving close to the shore at night, but have no idea what they could be doing. Elsewhere, George McGavin attaches a miniature tracking device to a vampire bat to discover whose blood it is feeding on.(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Sylvia Syms revisits archive interviews with some of Britain's biggest stars of the 1960s, including Sean Connery, George Lazenby, Peter Sellers, Peter O'Toole and Julie Christie.(n)

programme.title: The Titfield Thunderbolt
programme.desc: Villagers try to save their railway branch from closure by taking it over as a public business - but the owners of the local bus company try to sabotage their plans, forcing them to resort to more underhand methods. Ealing comedy, starring Stanley Holloway, George Relph, John Gregson, Godfrey Tearle and Sid James.(n)

credits.actor: Stanley Holloway, George Relph, John Gregson, Godfrey Tearle, Sid James

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Jonathan Pratt search for items to sell at auction at the Boconnoc estate in the Cornish countryside. Meanwhile, Paul Martin visits the gardens of Caerhays.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty, Jordan Stephens, Sally Lindsay and David O'Doherty test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games.(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: A wolf cub's life hangs in the balance after it is attacked by the rest of the pack. Elsewhere, Ben Fogle meets his old nemesis, Rio the cockatoo.(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Mediterranean Adventure
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the culinary delights of Provence in southern France. Starting out in Marseille, they meet a Moroccan woman who runs a welcome centre where immigrants can eat together - and who makes a fish stew to die for. Travelling west, they learn about the culinary influence of the Romans who conquered Europe 2,000 years ago, try a breakfast of pastry filled with spicy octopus, and dine with a Catalan farmer and his family on a regional speciality of barbecued snails.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: As the competition continues, chefs Shauna Froydenlund, Paul Cunningham, Leigh Ferguson and Alex Greene compete in the heats for Northern Ireland. Each chef prepares a starter and fish course celebrating iconic food from children's literature, including an ambitious duck crumble with rhubarb starter in ode to Alice in Wonderland, and a fish dish of halibut with oysters and foraged nettles and seaweed, inspired by Enid Blyton's The Magical Faraway Tree.(n)

programme.title: The Countess and the Russian Billionaire
programme.desc: An inside look at the secret world of Sergei Pugachev and his British partner Countess Alexandra Tolstoy. A hugely rich and powerful couple, with three beautiful children, homes across the world, and unimaginable wealth, the couple appeared to have it all, but their fairy tale life is about to come crashing down around them.(n)

programme.title: Mister Winner
programme.desc: Leslie's luck appears to be on the up when his mother's boyfriend invites him to interview for a job at a posh law firm. Leslie's preparation for the interview is meticulous and he has played the whole interview out in his head, but when he arrives at the reception he realise he has forgotten one very important thing, his shoes.(n)

programme.title: The Mini Mash Report
programme.desc: Nish Kumar and a cast of comedy correspondents present the satirical and surreal take on everything that has - and hasn't - happened in the news this week.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Sara Davies, Touker Suleyman, Deborah Meaden, Tej Lalvani and Peter Jones consider three more money-making proposals. The ideas under deliberation are an environmentally friendly range of plastic-free water bottles made from starch, ergonomic underwear, and an armchair tray for elderly people. Evan Davis presents.(n)

programme.title: BBC World News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Francesco's Mediterranean Voyage
programme.desc: Francesco da Mosto reaches the former Turkish capital, Istanbul, a city founded as the centrepiece of the former Ottoman Empire, bestriding the Bosphorus Strait. After touring the Topkapi Palace and an underground city, he tries selling rugs at the bazaar before taking a relaxing Turkish bath and massage.(n)

programme.title: Dynasties
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates footage of a tiger in the dense jungles of India's Bandhavgarh Reserve. The big cat has four newborn cubs to protect and is the dominant presence in a prime hunting territory. However, rival tigers are determined to take over, and one of her own previous offspring becomes one of the most persistent enemies the mother and her cubs face.(n)

programme.title: Du Fu: China's Greatest Poet
programme.desc: Michael Wood travels in the footsteps of the eighth-century poet, from the Yellow River to the Yangtze Gorges and down to the forested hills of Hunan. Born in 712, the age of Beowulf in Britain, Du Fu lived through the violent fall of China's Tang dynasty and as rebel armies sacked the capital and floods and famine wrecked the country, he was forced to flee, taking his family on the roads as refugees. With readings by Ian McKellen.(n)

programme.title: Yellow Is Forbidden
programme.desc: Documentary following Chinese fashion designer Guo Pei as she aims to switch from dressing her country's elite to trying to conquer the world of haute couture in Paris.(n)

programme.title: Horse People with Alexandra Tolstoy
programme.desc: Equestrian and adventurer Alexandra Tolstoy embarks on a globe-trotting quest to explore a variety of worldwide cultures, all of which rely on relationships with horses to survive in the unique climates of their homelands. This first edition sees Alexandra brave the icy tundras of Siberia, where she encounters Yakut horses specially bred to resist the extreme cold.(n)

programme.title: Handmade
programme.desc: Filmed over five days, this programme examines the complex and time-consuming process involved in building a classic Windsor chair, made by craftsman Jim Steele in his Warwickshire workshop. The traditional design features woods chosen for their different qualities - ash, elm and yew - and the finished object has just two screws and no glue.(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of Books
programme.sub-title: The Secret Life of Books: Confessions of an English Opium Eater
programme.desc: Performance poet John Cooper Clarke explores Thomas De Quincey's autobiographical work, discovering how his fellow Mancunian's addiction memoir avoids the cliches of modern 'misery-lit' in favour of something much more unsentimental and psychologically complex.(n)

programme.title: Bacchus Uncovered: Ancient God of Ecstasy
programme.desc: Professor Bettany Hughes investigates the story of Bacchus, travelling to Georgia, Britain, Jordan and Greece to discover his origins and his presence in the modern world.(n)

programme.title: Yellow Is Forbidden
programme.desc: Documentary following Chinese fashion designer Guo Pei as she aims to switch from dressing her country's elite to trying to conquer the world of haute couture in Paris.(n)

With readings by Ian McKellen.(n)

programme.title: Weird Nature
programme.desc: Exploring incredible creatures capable of amazing feats using just their bodies, beginning with a look at the common pearl caterpillar that can roll itself into a ball and wheel along at speed, and bushbabies that bounce along like rubber balls.(n)

programme.title: Digging for Britain: The Greatest Discoveries
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts re-examines key archaeological sites of prehistoric Britain.(n)

programme.title: The Art Mysteries
programme.sub-title: The Art Mysteries with Waldemar Januszczak
programme.desc: The story behind The Card Players, one of Cezanne's most famous artworks. Waldemar Januszczak considers how this image of two old men hunched over their game of cards expresses the artist's puzzling religious beliefs, particularly his fascination for Mary Magdalene.(n)

programme.title: Britain Beneath Your Feet
programme.desc: In the second of a two-part documentary about the fascinating world down below, Dallas Campbell reveals what goes on underground to keep the country on the move. He meets the hidden army of workers who keep Britain running, from the drivers of the largest tunnelling machines in the world to the engineers running a vast power station beneath a mountain in Wales. He also discovers how a secret wartime pipeline is now delivering fuel to Heathrow Airport, and helps to relocate a group of badgers who are threatening the foundations of a primary school.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Ancient Capital: Secrets of Orkney
programme.sub-title: Secrets of Orkney: Britain's Ancient Capital
programme.desc: Andy Torbet and local seafaring volunteers build a boat made of just willow and cow hide, and set out to cross the dangerous Pentland Firth as the ancient Orcadians would have done. Neil Oliver investigates the extraordinary discovery of some human bones, Chris Packham goes in search of whales and Shini Somara uncovers the powers of the tides.(n)

programme.title: Horse People with Alexandra Tolstoy
programme.desc: The avid equestrian continues her tour of the world's horse-powered communities by heading for Jerez in Andalucia. Here, she explores a culture often overshadowed by the Spanish tourism industry. She meets a family of mounted bullfighters, investigates pioneering horsebreeding techniques, and takes a dressage lesson that exceeds her expectations.(n)

programme.title: Handmade
programme.desc: Filmed in an industrial forge lit only by the welding torch and burning metal, this programme examines the process involved in the crafting of a steel knife. Bladesmith Owen Bush uses a variety of techniques, both ancient and modern, and takes his time over the complex skill of shaping and blending the steel into a knife.(n)

programme.title: Mark Gatiss on John Minton: The Lost Man of British Art
programme.desc: The League of Gentlemen star Mark Gatiss examines the life and career of largely forgotten English artist John Minton, who was one of the leading figures in post-war British art.(n)

programme.title: The Art Mysteries

programme.title: Handmade in Japan
programme.desc: The production of the traditional garment, following the painstaking process of the silk being bound, hand-dyed, woven and finally turned into a kimono by a seamstress.(n)

programme.title: Six Wives with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: Lucy witnesses the impact of Anne Boleyn on Henry VIII's affections, uncovering private moments that were reported by eyewitnesses or recorded in historical documents. The key moments during their marriage are recreated, as Anne falls from favour in court and is eventually executed, despite protestations of innocence regarding the rumours of her infidelity. As soon as Anne is dead, Henry marries Jane Seymour, who fulfils her most important role as Queen - giving birth to the healthy son that Henry has craved for so long.(n)

programme.title: Secret Universe: The Hidden Life of the Cell
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of what happens to a single human cell as it comes under attack from a virus, demonstrating how it defends itself from the hostile organism. The film uses CGI to show the membrane that acts as a security system, the remarkable turbines that power the cellular world, the nucleus housing DNA and the construction of thousands of different proteins all with unique tasks. David Tennant narrates.(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Predicting what may happen when the next flu pandemic occurs, stemming from a virus originating in the stomach of a dead duck. The Government's Influenza Contingency Plan highlights the possibility of the strain wiping out up to 750,000 people in Britain, regardless of health, and overwhelming hospitals and emergency services in the process.(n)

programme.title: Horse People with Alexandra Tolstoy
programme.desc: The globe-trotting adventurer draws her quest to a close in the western American state of Montana, where the horse is still an important part of the culture. She meets modern-day cowboys at a traditional ranch, where she witnesses horse-whispering and takes part in a barrel race. Later, she joins Native American herders as they head to round up wild buffalo in the mountains.(n)

But can a boy really wear a dress - and what will his headmaster, dad and friends in the football team think if they find out? Billy Kennedy stars as Dennis, with a supporting cast including Jennifer Saunders, Tim McInnerny, Meera Syal, Steve Speirs, Felicity Montagu, James Buckley and Walliams himself, with a cameo appearance by supermodel Kate Moss.(n)

programme.title: Our Girl
programme.desc: Georgie realises her smartwatch is still in a vehicle stolen by Dr Bahil's captors and can be tracked. Georgie learns more about the circumstances of Elvis' death, leaving her more determined than ever to see Omar brought to justice. programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Coronavirus: The Most at Risk - Panorama
programme.desc: More than one and a half million people have been told they have to stay at home for the next three months at least because they are most at risk from coronavirus, with the Government promising to do whatever it takes to support them. Richard Bilton hears from some of the most vulnerable about how their lives have changed and the people trying to help them.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian takes great pleasure in kicking Dotty out but he may regret his actions when she realises what he has done and plans her next move. A drunken Billy helps Linda and Mick put their problems into perspective, Chantelle throws herself into work with the Panesars, and Bobby develops a crush on Dotty.(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedian Jo Brand, singer Emma Bunton, athlete Jonnie Peacock and actor Shaun Williamson join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As ever, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory.(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The semi-finals begin with a spectacular challenge as the nine remaining contestants head to the Royal Navy base in Portsmouth, where they have to cook for HMS Diamond's Trafalgar Night celebration dinner. Split into three teams, they face the enormous challenge of mass catering at fine-dining level from the ship's galley. They then return to the MasterChef kitchen, where they must create a dish to hold onto their hard-fought place in the competition, with two being sent home at the end by judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace.(n)

programme.desc: The Wildlife Rescue Centre is exceptionally busy in spring when many newborns are orphaned or injured, and the staff members often take them home to feed them through the night. At Ardene House near Aberdeen, vet Ann Wortley gets out her power tools as she operates on a dog's knee to cure his lameness. And in Wick, vet Kiri Harvey is checking out Jock, a cute nine-week-old kitten with a suspected broken leg.(n)

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.sub-title: Britain's Best Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter embarks on her favourite countryside walk through the rolling plains of Cheshire, a trail with eight counties on show, two castles to explore and spectacular views across to Wales and Liverpool. A popular route for dog-walkers and sports, her trek proves to be full of surprises.(n)

programme.title: Tales from the Coast with Robson Green
programme.desc: The actor meets people who share his passion for the British coast, beginning with north Devon, a 90-mile stretch of shoreline that holds fond childhood memories for him. Robson starts his adventures by visiting the popular seaside resort of Ilfracombe, a town that stills attracts thousands of visitors every summer, to experience the classic British day out by the sea. After spending a night under canvas, he takes a boat ride to the port of Clovelly, a village that evokes the character and atmosphere of a bygone age, and visits the island of Lundy.(n)

programme.title: Return to Belsen
programme.desc: Jonathan Dimbleby returns to the site of the Bergen-Belsen concentration camp on the 75th anniversary of the liberation of the survivors by British troops. Jonathan's father was with the Allied forces as a reporter, and his account was one of the first to bring the full horrors of the Holocaust to the world's attention. Featuring contributions from survivors, liberators, and their children.(n)

programme.title: Belgravia

Susan makes a seemingly miraculous discovery, and begrudgingly agrees to accompany Anne and James to their country estate in the hope of extracting further information about Oliver. Stephen makes a last-ditch attempt to secure the funds he needs to pay off his debt - a deal that puts his life in danger.(n)

programme.title: Barging Round Britain with John Sergeant
programme.desc: The broadcaster returns to travel more of Britain's canal network, exploring the extraordinary stories of the people and places on the way. The series starts with John navigating the Peak Forest Canal, which runs through stunning countryside from Buxworth in Derbyshire to Ashton-under-Lyne. Along the way, in Hyde he meets former boxer Ricky Hatton, who discusses his plans to revitalise the area and offer new opportunities to local youngsters, and joins a team of junior archaeologists at Mellor Mill, who are attempting to unearth the remains of what was once one of the world's largest mills.(n)

programme.title: The Trouble with Maggie Cole
programme.desc: As the drama concludes, Roxanna is terrified by Alex's behaviour the previous night, and tries desperately to get to Alex before he confronts the money lenders. As everyone gathers for the village celebrations, a sudden shocking series of events are put in motion that could potentially cost the life of one beloved villager. Starring Dawn French, Mark Heap and Gwyneth Keyworth.(n)

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.sub-title: Britain's Best Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter embarks on her favourite countryside walk through the rolling plains of Cheshire, a trail with eight counties on show, two castles to explore and spectacular views across to Wales and Liverpool. A popular route for dog-walkers and sports, her trek proves to be full of surprises.(n)

programme.title: Tales from the Coast with Robson Green
programme.desc: The actor meets people who share his passion for the British coast, beginning with north Devon, a 90-mile stretch of shoreline that holds fond childhood memories for him. Robson starts his adventures by visiting the popular seaside resort of Ilfracombe, a town that stills attracts thousands of visitors every summer, to experience the classic British day out by the sea. After spending a night under canvas, he takes a boat ride to the port of Clovelly, a village that evokes the character and atmosphere of a bygone age, and visits the island of Lundy.(n)

programme.title: Return to Belsen
programme.desc: Jonathan Dimbleby returns to the site of the Bergen-Belsen concentration camp on the 75th anniversary of the liberation of the survivors by British troops. Jonathan's father was with the Allied forces as a reporter, and his account was one of the first to bring the full horrors of the Holocaust to the world's attention. Featuring contributions from survivors, liberators, and their children.(n)

programme.desc: Having previously toured their own countries, Gordon Ramsay, Gino D'Acampo and Fred Sirieix now hit the highways of of the States - although in the first episode, they are in Mexico, on a mission to taste the world's best breakfast. Along the way, the trio take part in a chili-eating competition, have a beach buggy race and watch some traditional Mexican wrestling - but it isn't long before they are persuaded not take part.(n)

programme.title: Kate & Koji
programme.desc: Despite Kate being sued after an accident in the cafe, she refuses to back down and Koji is reluctant to get involved. Although Medium is convinced she should just apologise and get it over with, Kate suspects the legal threats may have something to do with Councillor Bone. Comedy, starring Brenda Blethyn and Jimmy Akingbola.(n)

programme.title: Belgravia
programme.desc: John appeals to members of the Trenchards' staff in his bid to uncover the truth about Charles, while Turton and Ellis are quick to betray their employers and prove surprisingly adept at deceit and spying. Susan makes a seemingly miraculous discovery, and begrudgingly agrees to accompany Anne and James to their country estate in the hope of extracting further information about Oliver. Stephen makes a last-ditch attempt to secure the funds he needs to pay off his debt - a deal that puts his life in danger.(n)

programme.title: Barging Round Britain with John Sergeant
programme.desc: The broadcaster returns to travel more of Britain's canal network, exploring the extraordinary stories of the people and places on the way. The series starts with John navigating the Peak Forest Canal, which runs through stunning countryside from Buxworth in Derbyshire to Ashton-under-Lyne. Along the way, in Hyde he meets former boxer Ricky Hatton, who discusses his plans to revitalise the area and offer new opportunities to local youngsters, and joins a team of junior archaeologists at Mellor Mill, who are attempting to unearth the remains of what was once one of the world's largest mills.(n)

As everyone gathers for the village celebrations, a sudden shocking series of events are put in motion that could potentially cost the life of one beloved villager. programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps in a show that reveals what former PM David Cameron got up to behind the bins at Chequers. Plus, the host reveals the reason why Lorraine Kelly is testing Ross King's thongs.(n)

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Amy turns a boring Christmas Eve at Cloud 9 into a holiday adventure. Meanwhile, Jonah and Garrett's roommate issues boil over.(n)

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Dina and Cheyenne snoop through the staff members' social media feeds after a controversial video is posted. Glenn suspects Mateo is guilty of fraud.(n)

programme.title: Dinner Date
programme.desc: Saidat from London chooses three blind dates from a selection of five potential partners, based entirely on menus they have created.(n)

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Three singletons go up against each other in the ultimate shopping challenge, as they try to win the affections of picky photographer Danielle by each buying her an outfit.(n)

programme.title: Catchphrase

After the first elimination, the two remaining players battle to reach the Super Catchphrase round, where one of them could win £50,000 for their chosen charity.(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill identifies more celebrity lookalikes in a comical selection of viewer-submitted videos, featuring doppelgangers of Matthew Kelly, Steven Spielberg and Osama bin Laden, plus the rare and moving sight of a car giving birth.(n)

programme.desc: Oprah Winfrey joins the host, while Lil Nas X performs Panini.(n)

programme.title: Dinner Date
programme.desc: Will from Essex is given a chance to choose three dates from a pool of five potential sweethearts, inviting them for dinner to determine which of them he would like to see again.(n)

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: In Cardiff, three singletons compete in a series of shopping challenges to win the affections of sweet shop worker Jess. To impress her, they have to dress her. Jess has no information to go on but the outfits bought, so which of the three competitors will she choose to take on a date?(n)

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: The latest group of men to enter the 'love lift' are a Bruno Mars impersonator, a duck lover, a serial dater and a skip driver, who pull out all the stops in the hope of impressing 30 single women and winning a date - but must first get the female participants to keep their lights on as a sign of approval. Paddy McGuinness presents.(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill reveals how the craze of 'face change' is rendering photo identification useless, marvels at a performance by One Direction lookalikes and presents one of the most horrific videos seen on the show. Comic clips, featuring celebrity doppelgangers, below-the-belt mishaps and painful-looking accidents.(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill identifies more celebrity lookalikes in a comical selection of viewer-submitted videos, featuring doppelgangers of Abi Titmuss, Nadine Dorries and Roman Abramovic.(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring home-made bombs, an emu stealing a smartphone, and tips on how not to trim a tree. Plus, Holly Willoughby and Phillip Schofield lookalikes in an amusing situation.(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill introduces more videos, including a bride dealing with language difficulties, a woman getting run over by a fire engine, a dog that steals a smartphone and shoots a video with it, and a surprising discovery inside an egg.(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Walden is furious with Alan, thinking the relationship with Ms McMartin might jeopardise Louis' pending adoption. Furious, he joins a support group for adoptive fathers and discovers that it is a nice getaway from Alan. Comedy, starring Jon Cryer and Ashton Kutcher.(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Lyndsey tells Alan she is moving away for a fresh start, but despite her plans to leave, the pair end up in bed together, forcing Alan juggle two women once again.(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg is thrilled to be recruited as a model - but not everything is as it seems with the job. Meanwhile, Brian accompanies Stewie on a road trip adventure when the baby decides he wants to experience life outside of preschool.(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter discovers his first name is actually Justin while creating a checking account, and takes on the new moniker to create a younger, hipper alter-ego. The father then holds a party, where Quagmire unwittingly gets into trouble with his own mother.(n)

programme.title: Joel Dommett: Finding Emo
programme.desc: The comedian tries to re-form the emo band he played in when he was 15 in the hope that they can perform the final song they never played at their one-and-only gig.(n)

programme.desc: A burglar dubbed 'The Creeper' targets the villages of Midsomer as Barnaby and Jones investigate the murder of a guest at an upper-crust dinner party. programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: A mysterious fire that killed an artist with links to political activists leaves the police baffled, while Bright finds himself short-staffed when a stomach bug puts most of his men on sick leave. The situation becomes more serious when a housewife dies of the same condition. Endeavour investigates a family-run supermarket, believing the infection may have stemmed from their stock being deliberately contaminated, and discovers dark secrets that may have a connection to the unexplained blaze.(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: The duo continue to investigate one of the biggest and darkest cases they have ever faced. In the second edition, Rachel's safety is thrown into question when the killers change the rules of their sick game. Meanwhile, as a police officer, Janet is rendered powerless to protect her daughter, but she knows she must try to find a way to stop the madness. The crime drama returns for a fifth series, this time featuring a single crime throughout three parts. Starring Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones.(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: Sidney receives a series of anonymous phone calls, which he realises may be linked to a series of murders in which the victims were all important community figures killed by someone who leaves dead birds as a grisly calling card. The threat forces Sidney to question whether he can reconcile his relationship with Amanda and his calling as a vicar. Leonard bonds with Hilary as she cares for her dying father, while Geordie's investigation of the killings leads him to a mysterious home for people with disabilities. Detective drama set in the 1950s, starring James Norton and Robson Green.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The murder of Midsomer resident and biscuit factory boss Eric Calder leads Barnaby and Nelson to Copenhagen. Anglo-Danish collaboration originally shown to mark Midsomer's 100th episode, guest starring Ann Eleonora Jorgensen (The Killing), Birgitte Hjort Sorensen (Borgen), Sanjeev Bhaskar and Joanna Scanlan, alongside Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee.(n)

Crime drama, starring Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones.(n)

programme.title: Grantchester

After the match, a buffet ends badly when contaminated beer leaves everyone feeling ill, a matter Geoff is keen for Geordie to cover up. However, a worse fate awaits Zafar, who is later found to have received a fatal dose of the poison his teammates and spectators had received.(n)

programme.desc: Richard Abernethie is found dead shortly after disinheriting his sole heir and specifying that his legacy is to be divided equally among the remaining family members. programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Anti-fur protester Sue Padgett finds herself in deep trouble when her boyfriend and fellow crusader Nick Payton starts a fire at a haulier's depot. Carol and Chris fall out when they offer opposing advice to a pregnant teenager, while Peggy enthusiastically prepares David for a date with a farmer's ample daughter Tina Bestwick. programme.desc: The shock of Luke's death is felt around the village. Nellie suspects that Luke may have been trying to kill Tina. The police question Tina again about the accident.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A burglar dubbed 'The Creeper' targets the villages of Midsomer as Barnaby and Jones investigate the murder of a guest at an upper-crust dinner party. As the case progresses, Barnaby becomes the thief's latest victim and it emerges that several high-society friends with connections to gangsters and a police chief have a few secrets worth killing for. Detective drama, starring John Nettles and Jason Hughes, with guest appearances from Rik Mayall and Jenny Agutter.(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: A mysterious fire that killed an artist with links to political activists leaves the police baffled, while Bright finds himself short-staffed when a stomach bug puts most of his men on sick leave. The situation becomes more serious when a housewife dies of the same condition. Endeavour investigates a family-run supermarket, believing the infection may have stemmed from their stock being deliberately contaminated, and discovers dark secrets that may have a connection to the unexplained blaze.(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: The duo continue to investigate one of the biggest and darkest cases they have ever faced. In the second edition, Rachel's safety is thrown into question when the killers change the rules of their sick game. Meanwhile, as a police officer, Janet is rendered powerless to protect her daughter, but she knows she must try to find a way to stop the madness. The crime drama returns for a fifth series, this time featuring a single crime throughout three parts. Starring Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones.(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: Sidney receives a series of anonymous phone calls, which he realises may be linked to a series of murders in which the victims were all important community figures killed by someone who leaves dead birds as a grisly calling card. The threat forces Sidney to question whether he can reconcile his relationship with Amanda and his calling as a vicar. Leonard bonds with Hilary as she cares for her dying father, while Geordie's investigation of the killings leads him to a mysterious home for people with disabilities. Detective drama set in the 1950s, starring James Norton and Robson Green.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot

programme.desc: The murder of Midsomer resident and biscuit factory boss Eric Calder leads Barnaby and Nelson to Copenhagen. After the match, a buffet ends badly when contaminated beer leaves everyone feeling ill, a matter Geoff is keen for Geordie to cover up. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Richard Abernethie is found dead shortly after disinheriting his sole heir and specifying that his legacy is to be divided equally among the remaining family members. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Robert Bathurst, Anna Calder-Marshall and Geraldine James.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie's girlfriend is critically injured in a restaurant bomb blast, prompting him to seek revenge. When it transpires the intended target was an apparently unimportant accountant, Bodie ignores Cowley's orders and begins probing the man's past. Lewis Collins stars.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan gets caught in the crossfire between the family of a recently deceased gangland leader and the members of the late man's crime syndicate, regarding the division of the hoodlum's wealth. Hywel Bennett guest stars, with John Thaw.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan encounters a former nemesis at a party, who drunkenly admits involvement in an earlier robbery in which he was let go due to lack of evidence. programme.title: Rambo: First Blood Part II
programme.desc: Incarcerated Special Forces veteran John Rambo is offered the chance for freedom by former commander Colonel Trautman if he takes up the offer to return to the jungles of Vietnam on a life-or-death mission to rescue American PoWs. During the course of the operation, he develops strong feelings for his guide, a female freedom fighter - but tragedy strikes, leaving him more determined than ever to exact a bloody and explosive revenge. Action adventure sequel, starring Sylvester Stallone, Richard Crenna, Julia Nickson-Soul, Steven Berkoff, George Kee Cheung, Andy Wood and Martin Kove.(n)

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Riggs and Murtaugh are forced once more to work with DEA agent Karen Palmer in order to protect Leo Getz, an ambulance-chasing attorney linked to the cartel. As the three delve further into the case, they begin to realise that Leo may not be the only one with a target on his back. Meanwhile, Riggs begins to view Agent Palmer in a new light, while Murtaugh is left worried after Trish makes a spontaneous decision. Damon Wayans, Clayne Crawford and Hilarie Burton star in this fast-paced series, based on the hit, big-screen movie.(n)

programme.title: Only When I Laugh

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: Part one of two. The coroner suffers pre-wedding nerves, a feeling heightened by his investigation into the case of a woman who claims to have killed her husband.(n)

programme.title: Kojak

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: A woman is tormented by calls from her supposedly deceased husband, whose lifeless body was pulled from the sea several weeks earlier. programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur gets his hands on the unreleased recording of a late, lamented pop star's final song, and decides to launch himself as a music-biz entrepreneur. Mel Smith guest stars, with George Cole and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle have reason to suspect their former mentor has turned 'rogue' for money after two key suspects in a drug-smuggling case are delivered to their killers. Action drama, with Lewis Collins, Martin Shaw and Glyn Owen.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A missing small-time crook turns up dead, leading to a murder investigation - but Carter's girlfriend develops a peculiar interest in the proceedings. Geraldine James guest stars, with Dennis Waterman and John Thaw.(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: Part two of two. The coroner's marriage plans are put on hold while he continues the investigation into the case of a woman who claims to have killed her husband.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A detective thinks he has been passed over for promotion once too often, and interferes in a case Theo is working on to boost his own career. Final episode of the detective drama.(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar has to find out whether a cool blonde is a secret agent or a crook - and if she could do him harm. Sixties spy adventure, directed by and starring Roger Moore.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur's prestige as a pillar of the community is boosted by an invitation to address a meeting of his local Rotary Club - which is being held in the very hotel where Terry is acting as temporary house detective. However, a pair of thieves posing as priests and a mangeress equally as crooked as Arthur threaten to ruin his grand moment. Drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.sub-title: Not a Very Civil Civil Servant
programme.desc: As whole estates of houses begin to crumble, CI5 uncovers corruption surrounding the awarding of contracts to build homes. Maurice Denham and Bill Fraser guest star alongside Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A crook arranges the conversion of replica firearms into the real thing and sells them on to small-time hoodlums. When one of the arms misfires killing both a policeman and its holder, Regan is intent on discovering the source before more innocent lives are lost. Crime drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as another four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser. Answering general knowledge questions, they each add as much money as they dare to the jackpot - but anyone caught by the Chaser is out for good, leaving fewer players to team up and play in the final chase.(n)

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. A series of VIPs travel through Heathrow Airport terminals. Meanwhile, a team of hockey champions try to use their gold medals to get an upgrade.(n)

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. Officer Estefania pushes passengers through security with a unique handling method, while drugs dog Cole works to protect the border.(n)

programme.title: Under Siege 2: Dark Territory
programme.desc: A retired Navy veteran travelling by train is forced to leap into action when he realises terrorist hijackers are on board. Their leader, a vengeful former government operative with access to a powerful orbital weapon, is threatening to set off a chain of earthquakes if his demand for $1billion is not met. Action adventure sequel, with Steven Seagal, Eric Bogosian, Morris Chestnut, Brenda Bakke and Katherine Heigl.(n)

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Only When I Laugh
programme.desc: An MP renowned for his stance against the cost of the NHS is admitted to the men's ward for treatment, causing much unrest for the long-suffering patients.(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: An illiterate man is killed in an industrial accident because he was unable to read the warning signs, so Quincy sets out to investigate the tragic accident.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.sub-title: May the Horse Be with You
programme.desc: A retired cop who has been secretly housing racehorses in the NYPD stables is injured when he tries to prevent the theft of one of his valuable animals. Theo sets out to track down the culprit as the police are made aware of the situation. Detective drama, starring Telly Savalas.(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: The Saint is framed for the murder of a young sculptor, and with the help of the victim's girlfriend, Chief Inspector Teal fights to clear his name and uncover the real culprit. Adventure, starring Roger Moore.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Members of the local travelling community are blamed when a car-dealer friend of Arthur's has the frighteners put on him. However, they soon prove useful allies when those actually responsible are revealed. Comedy drama, guest starring Jimmy Nail and June Whitfield.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie's girlfriend is critically injured in a restaurant bomb blast, prompting him to seek revenge. When it transpires the intended target was an apparently unimportant accountant, Bodie ignores Cowley's orders and begins probing the man's past. Lewis Collins stars.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan gets caught in the crossfire between the family of a recently deceased gangland leader and the members of the late man's crime syndicate, regarding the division of the hoodlum's wealth. The hitherto innocent son manages to play-off his parent's ex-cohorts against one another with increasing dexterity and appears only too keen to pick up the duplicitous traits of his late father. Hywel Bennett guest stars, with John Thaw.(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: A psychic housewife is enlisted to help Quincy track down a crazed sex-attacker and the murderer of several young women, putting her own life in danger.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A paparazzi photographer becomes the prime suspect in a jewel theft and the phony kidnapping of a former actress, and helps Theo to bring the real criminals to justice.(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar joins forces with a glamorous female agent in order to help liberate John Spring, a double agent whom the Russians believe to be working for them. Roger Moore stars.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.sub-title: If Money Be the Food of Love, Play On
programme.desc: Arthur and Terry fall under the spell of a bewitching Australian beauty who has ventured from Down Under in search of her missing fiance. However, she may just happen to be concealing an ulterior motive. George Cole and Dennis Waterman star.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle begin an investigation into why a city's crime rate is steadily falling. They later find themselves victimised by local officers after being charged with a minor traffic offence. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan encounters a former nemesis at a party, who drunkenly admits involvement in an earlier robbery in which he was let go due to lack of evidence. He disapproves of his daughter's villainous boyfriend, and Regan suspects he is being exploited to help break the romantic union. However, an even darker plan is being hatched, with the daughter an innocent pawn in a robbery. Kenny Lynch and Derek Francis guest star, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught.(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.sub-title: Killer from the Abyss - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. Jeremy Wade takes on the biggest investigation of his career - to unravel what happened to the passengers of the RMS Laconia, torpedoed in the mid-Atlantic.(n)

programme.title: Giant Lobster Hunters
programme.desc: Peter MacKenzie, skipper of the Triton, looks to add to his season's haul of 2.5 tonnes, and the all-in price of lobsters spikes to more than $100 per kilogramme.(n)

programme.title: The Krays
programme.desc: Biopic of the infamous twin brothers who built up a criminal empire in the East End of London during the 1960s. Seizing territory from an already established rival by force, they quickly rose to prominence in the city's gangland culture. However, over time the brothers began to clash, while enemies stood ready to bring them down. Starring Gary and Martin Kemp, Billie Whitelaw and Tom Bell.(n)

The programme also looks at Putin's decision to annex Crimea and launch a war in eastern Ukraine and Russia's interference in politics on both sides of the Atlantic and in the Middle East.(n)

programme.title: Dispatches
programme.sub-title: Coronavirus: How Britain Is Changing - Dispatches
programme.desc: As coronavirus takes hold of Britain, Dispatches investigates the long-term effects of the outbreak and asks how the deadly Covid-19 is changing Britain in the long term. As the death toll rises, the programme looks at everything from seismic changes in economic policy to shifts in the way we view and treat other people, and from a drop in pollution to the widening of the gaps between rich and poor, young and old. The programme asks what a post-Covid 19 Britain, torn apart by the disease, might look like, and the potential changes to the way people live their lives for years to come.(n)

programme.title: The Yorkshire Steam Railway: All Aboard
programme.desc: A summer heatwave brings record numbers to the railway, though joy turns to despair for the staff when it looks like the railway will be prevented from running its iconic steam engines through a tinder-dry national park. Through a series of expensive preventative measures - including hiring in a couple of heritage diesel trains and having a makeshift fire truck follow every engine - boss Chris manages to keep the business running. The engine shed workers are also kept busy trying to get star locomotive Eric steaming again.(n)

programme.title: Around the World by Train with Tony Robinson
programme.desc: The actor travels through the heart of South America, exploring Argentina and Peru. In Buenos Aires he meets up with charismatic local Viviana Parara. They go tango dancing, out for a lunch of prime Argentine steak and finally find themselves in the middle of a huge street dance. Tony then travels north into Peru, heading to the ancient city of Cusco where he takes a detour to visit the Amazon rainforest. A further train ride takes him from Cusco up to the ancient ruins of Machu Picchu. Visiting this Incan citadel has been a lifelong dream for Tony, and it does not disappoint.(n)

programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.title: Hard Men: Street Justice
programme.desc: Former detective Rod Carter reveals how a spate of more than 50 murders have emerged from a gang rivalry in Manchester that began over a borrowed leather jacket, exploring the tiny area of the city that has played host to many of the deaths. Former gang leader Matthew Norford explains how a fight over territory led to him being shot, while ex-enforcer Cody Lachey talks about how many drug gangs worked hand-in-hand with door staff to maximise their profits.(n)

programme.title: The Yorkshire Steam Railway: All Aboard
programme.desc: Preparations are under way for Yorkshire Day, including a special Yorkshire-themed Pullman service, with Gill and husband Paul getting hands-on sorting out the event. There is also another big occasion on the horizon and Tim is organising the wedding of head boilersmith Mark and his equally steam-mad fiancée Emma. Their love blossomed because of mutual passion for steam, and in particular the B1, so they want the engine to pull them on their wedding train, but issues with the engine means it might miss the big day.(n)

programme.title: Dogs with Extraordinary Jobs
programme.desc: A look at some working dogs. A German shepherd mix search-and-rescue dog and her Los Angeles firefighter partner search for survivors after a mudslide, while elsewhere spaniels use their noses to search for underground water leaks, with their human handlers.(n)

programme.title: The Yorkshire Vet
programme.desc: A rare cattle breeder fears for the life of his favourite cow, which has been diagnosed with cancer and will need major surgery to have any chance of survival. A rottweiler puppy becomes seriously ill after eating 15 stones, a ram undergoes fertility tests, and a donkey has a problem with one of its legs. Plus, receptionist Sylvia leaves the Skeldale practice, so the staff prepares a special surprise for her.(n)

programme.title: A Country Life for Half the Price with Kate Humble
programme.desc: Kate meets people who have started self-sufficient new lives in the country. The first episode features the Cook family from Exeter, who plan to move to the other end of the country and make a fresh start in the Shetland Islands. They plan to fund their new lifestyle through AirBnB and an isolated space for authors, but their move is complicated by a serious illness in the family.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Factories
programme.sub-title: Secrets of the Mr Kipling Factory
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes guide to the Mr Kipling Factory in Carlton, South Yorkshire, which first opened in the 1970s and was the largest bakery in Europe at the time. Members of the workforce provide an insight into the cutting edge technology that keeps the cakes coming 24 hours a day, while food historian Polly Russell presents a guide to the company's history, revealing how recipes that date back to the 1800s remain popular favourites to this day.(n)

programme.title: Too Fat to Fly
programme.desc: Documentary examining embarrassing incidents that obese Britons have suffered on holiday, and the effect the humiliation had on those involved. A couple who were told they were too heavy to sit next to each other on a flight to Guernsey have since lost 16st between them, while a woman changed her lifestyle and went from a size 22 to an 8 after being ridiculed by a masseuse in Turkey. Helen Dee, Colin Newell and Gemma Cruickshank are sent to road-test flights and investigate the problems facing today's larger fliers.(n)

programme.title: Little Britons
programme.sub-title: Drag Queen Dwarf & Other Little Britons
programme.desc: Observational documentary following people living with dwarfism. Jamie John, the UK's only dwarf drag queen, plans to perform at Edinburgh Fringe Festival. Meanwhile, cameras follow budding chef Louis from Worcester on work experience in a Michelin starred restaurant, and Mary from London, a fashion designer for people with dwarfism. Narrated by Warwick Davis.(n)

programme.title: The Yorkshire Steam Railway: All Aboard
programme.desc: The annual Gala Festival is staged, showcasing steam engines from up and down the country. As part of the events, the North York Moors Railway has agreed to swap its precious 100-year-old Q6 engine, the last example in the world, with an engine from the Severn Valley heritage railway. However, there is a problem as the Q6 is undergoing a two-year overhaul and is not ready. A crack has been found in the engine box, and there is no specialist steam copper welder available at short notice to fix it. If the Q6 cannot be sent to the Severn Valley, the entire festival may be ruined.(n)

programme.title: Sewermen
programme.desc: Docuseries following the everyday heroes who keep Britain's water systems flowing free. Steve and Michael tackle a stubborn Liverpool fatberg the size of four jumbo jets, while Mick and John try to stop a sewage leak from ruining Passover.(n)

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: First through Doctor Qamar's consulting room door this week is Brian, a regular patient who is concerned about memory loss and is desperate for some answers. Elsewhere, a young boy is diagnosed with balanitis, and doctor Wyne treats one of her regular patients, who has MS and a dependency on alcohol.(n)

programme.title: Casualty 24/7
programme.desc: There are complications when a woman who suffers from kidney failure is admitted with a heart condition, and two builders are rushed into casualty after a roof collapsed under them. Meanwhile, 65-year-old Alan goes for a CT scan after an epileptic fit left him with a serious head wound, and doctors treat a 12-year-old boy who has been attacked by a dog.(n)

programme.title: Sarah Payne: A Mother's Story
programme.desc: Documentary shedding light on the emotional fallout of the murder of Sarah Payne, the eight-year-old girl who was kidnapped and killed in Kingston Gorse, West Sussex in 2000. Two weeks after her disappearance, Sarah's body was found, and after a high-profile police investigation, Roy Whiting was convicted of her murder and sentenced to life imprisonment. Testimonies from friends, family, police officers, key witnesses and experts in criminology are combined with an interview with Sarah's mother, to illustrate the tragic toll the case took on those closest to the victim.(n)

programme.title: When Kids Kill
programme.desc: Leah Green presents this documentary investigating the case of 18-year-old Lewis Daynes, who exploited schoolboy Breck Bednar's career aspirations in order to groom him online before killing him. Leah speaks to Breck's mother, who is determined to improve other children's awareness of online dangers.(n)

programme.title: Punk Chef on the Road

programme.title: Rugrats Go Wild
programme.desc: A leaky boat leaves the precocious youngsters stranded on a jungle island, but their luck changes when explorers the Wild Thornberrys come to their rescue. However, Thornberry dad Nigel loses his memory, so it falls to his daughter Eliza to save the day. Children's animated adventure, with the voices of Nancy Cartwright, Bruce Willis, Lacey Chabert, Elizabeth Daily and Tim Curry.(n)

programme.title: Kubo and the Two Strings
programme.desc: A boy unwittingly summons an evil spirit to wreak havoc on his peaceful village home. The only hope of defeating the vengeful creature is to find a magical suit of armour belonging to the boy's father - but finding it will entail matching wits with an assortment of monstrous foes. Animated adventure, with the voices of Charlize Theron, Art Parkinson and Matthew McConaughey.(n)

programme.title: The Last Airbender

After 100 years, the chosen one is found and must find a way to restore harmony. Fantasy adventure based on the cartoon series, starring Noah Ringer and Dev Patel.(n)

programme.title: Paddington

He finds modern Britain to be very different from the stories told to him by his aunt, but makes a new home with a kindly family. However, a sinister taxidermist intends to turn him into a museum exhibit. Comedy based on Michael Bond's series of children's books, starring Hugh Bonneville, Sally Hawkins and Nicole Kidman, with the voice of Ben Whishaw.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners visit the Elegant House bed and breakfast in Liverpool on their second night, where host John Brady hopes his unique property will stand out from the crowd. programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit is to Monmouthshire, where Peter and Amanda Copp want to rope their guests into the world of 'glamping' at Hidden Valley Yurts. John has a hairy encounter in his yurt, and the shared bathroom facilities fail to impress David and Nikki. The next morning, the DIY breakfast proves underwhelming for some, while the hosts discover that the great outdoors is not everyone's cup of tea.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Former hotel inspector and owner David Stanbridge and manager Nikki Deans welcome the contestants to the Cley Hall Hotel in Spalding, Lincolnshire. Peter and Amanda are impressed by their room, but Simon and Lyn are unhappy with the state of the carpet. The next morning, the guests decide to put their eight-minute breakfast rule to the test, and some of them have concerns about Nikki's waitressing skills.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The hosts meet for the final time to find out what they've been paid and confront their critics. Simon and Lyn kick off proceedings by demanding answers from John about his disturbed night's sleep, but then find themselves taken to task by David over their cleanliness feedback. Tensions run high as the payments are revealed and the winner is announced.(n)

An octogenarian couple are rushed in to A&E after being knocked over by a motorcyclist while crossing the road, and the police maintain an active presence as paramedics bring in a man who has collapsed after a suspected drug overdose. Plus, an 88-year-old woman with a swollen leg discusses her ill-fated marriage to a much younger man nearly four decades ago.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at stately home Anstey Hall in Cambridge, with father and son team John and Johnny de Bruyne. The fun starts with a character-revealing afternoon of goose-herding, before the participants dress to impress at a formal dinner. John recounts stories of royal connections - which fails to impress hotel managers Mark and Layla. In the morning, as they tuck in to a self-service breakfast, guest Tim decides that DIY doesn't work at Anstey Hall, but when he notes this on his feedback form, John and Johnny accuse him of game-playing.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the competition is to clothes-free Clover Spa and Hotel in Birmingham, where owner Tim Higgs hopes to convert his rivals to the joys of naturism. Judging with an extra critical eye are John and Jonny De Bruyne, who received negative feedback from Tim, and a thorough room inspection reveals a dusty porn DVD. Tim tries to encourage his nervous guests to take part in a naked spa experience, but John opts to watch television instead. When feedback is invited, Tim is delighted to discover Mark and Layla would stay again but shocked by the 'porn film' discovery.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the Angel Hotel in Coleford, Gloucestershire for the third day of the competition, where a treasure hunt organised by hosts Mark and Layla brings out their guests' competitive sides. Meanwhile, there is confusion at breakfast and Layla's manners are criticised.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stay is at Higher Gitcombe in Totnes, Devon, where owners Paul and Lynnie Jolly set out to spoil their guests. Everyone is overwhelmed by the added extras, but naturist Tim is put off by cushions and clutter. After a masterclass in Nordic walking, Lynnie serves up a veg smoothie, which unhealthy eaters Mark and Layla dubiously down in one. Over dinner, Tim challenges his host's organic 'niche'. And Layla, still smarting from his negative feedback, labels him 'weird, naked Tim'. At breakfast, guests are wary as Tim's order goes wrong and Jonny and Layla engage in an eating competition. When feedback is invited, the Jollys are affronted by accusations of special treatment and vow to put the record straight.(n)

James and Richard pin their hopes on the pan-European rail network in a bid to secure the prize of tickets to a top-flight football match, with nothing but a long journey home awaiting the losers. On the test track, Jeremy puts Toyota's GT86 coupe through its paces, and its promise of entertaining rear-wheel-drive handling should put a smile on his face.
