programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: The team travels from Dublin to Derry. On the way, Miranda Krestovnikoff visits the only beach race in Europe run under Jockey Club rules at Laytown Races and Alice Roberts finds herself sent to the Irish salt mines. Mark Horton investigates a lost year in the life of the world's first ocean liner, while Neil Oliver meets the Londonderry women who secretly witnessed the surrender of German U-Boats at the end of the Second World War.(n)

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: Examining the 1966 murders of three police officers in Shepherd's Bush that led to a huge manhunt and calls for the death penalty to be reinstated.(n)

programme.title: Hitler's Propaganda Machine
programme.desc: As Hitler's ambitions spread beyond Germany, the world drew toward another great war. This one would be fought on many battlefields, but also for the hearts and minds of the people.(n)

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: How the UFO famously sighted at Roswell may have been an experimental aircraft developed by the Nazis during the Second World War. Plus, theories about Glenn Miller's disappearance.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A vast project in the icy wilderness of southern Alaska, and a landscape of confusing and surprising structures that represented a bold statement of Spain's plans for the future.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Railways
programme.sub-title: Kiruna to Narvik - Hitler's Arctic Railway Line
programme.desc: The Nordland Line, the longest in Norway at 453 miles. Stretching between Trondheim and Bodo, it was first built to transport the country's greatest natural resource - iron ore.(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: The changing landscape of the coastline from Newcastle to Hull. Neil Oliver explores the ancient coastal home of the Venerable Bede, and goes to Bridlington to learn about the 200-year struggle to design an unsinkable lifeboat. Mark Horton discovers how laser technology could predict coastal erosion, Dick Strawbridge finds out how Middlesbrough's Transporter Bridge put the city on the map, and Miranda Krestovnikoff heads to Bempton Cliffs.(n)

programme.title: Africa
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the vast and diverse continent, aiming to show its landscapes and wildlife as they have never been seen before. He begins in the Kalahari desert, Africa's south-west corner, where clever meerkats are outsmarted by a wily bird's use of mimicry, a solitary rhino is seen to exhibit previously unknown sociable behaviours, terrifying insects prey on baby birds and the rarest fish in the world is filmed for the first time. In the neighbouring Namib desert, which receives almost no rain, the competition for scant resources can be fierce, as proven by a ferocious fight for supremacy between two giraffes.(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: An insight into woodlands around the world, from the stunted conifers cloaked in snow and ice in the Arctic forests, to the broadleaf woods in North America and Europe. Also featured are the 100m-high giant redwoods of California, as well as Madagascar's unusual upside-down trees, which store water in their swollen trunks and harbour strange wildlife. Presented by David Attenborough.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Railways
programme.sub-title: The Liverpool to Manchester Line - The First Modern Railway
programme.desc: The Liverpool and Manchester Railway, which revolutionised rail travel when it opened in 1830, becoming the first railway to have a signalling system among many other things.(n)

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two
programme.desc: Documentary explaining how a squadron of low-flying bombers freed resistance fighters from Amiens Jail during the Second World War.(n)

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: Exploring UFO sightings over Canada and the US during both World Wars and theories that Churchill knew about the attack on Pearl Harbor before it happened.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering

programme.title: Africa
programme.desc: David Attenborough continues his exploration of the continent in east Africa, which is a haven for life and supports a higher density of large mammals than anywhere else on Earth. From dense forests to snow-capped peaks, steamy swamps to endless savannah, it's also a land of unpredictable extremes, where creatures must adapt to survive. In Tanzania, a tiny lizard risks its life to eat flies from the face of a sleepy lion. The nesting habits of the 4ft shoebill are also captured on camera - the giant bird has two chicks, but due to sibling rivalry only one will survive. On the plains of Amboseli, a baby elephant is near to death in the drought, so its mother leaves the herd to stay with her offspring until the bitter end.(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough observes some of the varied species to be found in tropical rainforests, from the amusing courtship rituals of the birds of paradise and a chimpanzee group defending its territory to a flying-frog mating orgy and the sinister parasitic fungi cordyceps, which eat their victims alive.(n)

programme.desc: Neil Oliver admires ginger seals at Walton-on-the-Naze, while Nick Crane examines the area's fossil record. The team also visits Felixstowe, the UK's busiest container port, where HM Revenue and Customs uncover a stash of cocaine smuggled among furniture.(n)

programme.title: Deadliest Crash: Disaster at Le Mans
programme.desc: Documentary examining what caused Pierre Levegh's Mercedes to crash into the crowd during the 1955 Le Mans 24-hour race, killing more than 80 people - an accident that remains the worst in the history of motor racing. Among the theories discussed are the use of untested technologies by the car's manufacturers, the addition of a lethal fuel additive and allegations of reckless driving by future Formula One world champion Mike Hawthorn, who won the race with Jaguar team partner Ivor Bueb. Narrated by Jack Deam.(n)

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: Part two of two. A re-examination of Jack the Ripper's crimes, revealing how the story known today was shaped by the sensationalist press of 1888. Featuring contributions from Ripper expert Donald Rumbelow and writer Hallie Rubenhold, author of The Five - the only book to tell the lives of the victims.(n)

programme.title: Africa
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the varied creatures of the Congo rainforest, one of the most competitive habitats on Earth. A sweet-toothed chimpanzee uses a complex array of tools to access honey from a beehive, the elusive picathartes bird feeds unsuspecting frogs to its dinosaur-like chicks and a massive female python reveals her maternal side as she sunbathes to incubate her eggs. Tiny leaf-folding frogs battle it out kung fu style, and as darkness falls the forest elephants gather to face their rivals.(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: Following the epic journey of a humpback whale and its calf as they travel from a tropical paradise to the species' great feeding ground in the icy polar seas. David Attenborough also observes footage of colonies of seabirds in the Arabian desert and surfing dolphins in Australia that swim onto the beach to catch their fish.(n)

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The history of pioneering modern-day mega structures, revealing that behind every seemingly impossible marvel of modern engineering is a cast of historic trailblazing engineers who designed new building techniques, took risks on untested materials and revolutionised their field. The first programme focuses on the aircraft carrier.(n)

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two
programme.desc: Stories of people who came close to losing their lives during the Second World War, beginning with tales about the Chindits, the army's elite jungle troops division who beat the Japanese in a daring mission.(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Acclaimed chronicle of the Second World War, beginning with an account of Hitler's ascent to power at a time when the Germans were stricken by humiliating military defeat, runaway inflation and economic depression. The Nazi leader's promises of regained national pride and prosperity struck a chord with millions of ordinary citizens, and he was elected on a tidal wave of popular support. Narrated by Laurence Olivier.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: The underground base with the power to destroy a nation, the site linked with an attempt to kill a vicious dictator, and an ancient world discovered in the open American wilderness.(n)

programme.title: Impossible Engineering

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletch plans to bring a bit of Christmas spirit to Slade Prison in the form of his own home-made booze. But when Mackay and Barrowclough uncover the stash in his and Godber's cell, the crafty con seems in danger of spending the yuletide period in solitary confinement. Festive edition of the classic comedy from 1976, starring Ronnie Barker and Fulton Mackay.(n)

programme.desc: The chef travels to Philadelphia to help failing Mexican restaurant Zocalo. Owners Greg and Mary Russell met and fell in love while they were working there, and the place held such sentimental importance to them they pooled their money and bought it. Now Gordon tries to turn things around before the floundering business ruins their marriage and tears their family apart.(n)

programme.desc: The temperatures soar as Dick and Angel face the busiest period of the year, as the school holidays coincide with peak wedding season. Dick comes up with an ingenious solution to stop wedding cakes from melting in the heat, but it involves piercing the chateau's walls. The moat is transformed into a boating lake, giving visitors a chance to cool down, and the family takes to the water in a home-made punt.(n)

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: With police numbers lower than they've been for decades, this edition focuses on the increased demands on front-line police in Northamptonshire. Call handlers are finding their work increasingly demoralising, while the squeeze on resources is not helped by the public's ever-broadening sense of what constitutes an emergency.(n)

programme.title: The Undateables
programme.desc: Revisiting three familiar faces who are still looking for love, and one former singleton who is now engaged and is getting married to his college sweetheart. Charlotte, who has autism, is moving out of her family home and is looking for a life partner. Meanwhile, train-lover Richard from Warwickshire is still searching for someone to share his life with, as is Alex, who has written his own book about autism since last appearing on the programme. Finally the programme catches up with 26-year-old Sam, who is preparing to marry his old friend from college, Vanessa.(n)

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer

For the signature round, they must prepare a batch of decorated traybakes, while the technical challenge involves a classic British staple. Finally, for the Showstopper, they must create a scene out of biscuits depicting what they wanted to be when they grew up.(n)

She selects a former rock guitarist, the owner of a Greek cafe, a talkative former car salesman, a cake-baking retired businessman, and a New Age vegan with a gift for tantric massage.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Beauty pageant queen Pippa Langhorne throws the second party in Worcestershire, and hopes to impress her fellow diners with a sophisticated Midsummer Night's Dream-themed event. However, her starter fails to win the approval of shellfish-hating Claire Wier, and Tim Lawson disapproves on the basis of his friendship with fish. Everyone is surprised as the hostess brings out an unusual dessert, and a guest appearance by her singing dog Buddy rounds off a memorable evening.(n)

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples from Swansea and Bridgend compete to throw the best dinner party. Kathy and Justin kick off the competition with an Italian-inspired menu, which is overshadowed by Paul's poor table manners and tactless banter. Mark and Maria are the next to host and serve their leftovers-inspired starter, chicken lettuce boats. Finally, it's time for Kathy to wreak revenge during Paul and Sarah's evening. Chaos in the kitchen ensues, culminating in a showdown at the table.(n)

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Robert and Amy move into Ray's basement in a bid to get some privacy, but they fail to account for Marie - who spoils their plans. Comedy, starring Ray Romano, Brad Garrett, Doris Roberts and Monica Horan.(n)

credits.actor: Ray Romano, Brad Garrett, Doris Roberts, Monica Horan

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra and Ray reminisce about how they met, recalling incidents such as Marie's first encounter with her future daughter-in-law. Comedy, starring Ray Romano.(n)

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The shrink takes everyone to a ski lodge for a weekend break, but wires are crossed and everyone gets the wrong impression about one another. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer.(n)

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Lilith (Bebe Neuwirth) arrives out of the blue and tries to rekindle her relationship with Frasier. He manages to resist her charms, but Niles is not as strong-willed. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce.(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Phil Spencer catches up with two couples who struggled to find their first marital home, including Rupreet and Sundeep who had their hearts set on a specific area in Harrow. Phil also revisits Paul and Elly, who searched for a property in Bristol, to see if they found the perfect house.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Phillip and Janet call on friends and family to help complete their bridal suite in time for their daughter's wedding, and Mariam and Johnno await the arrival of seven guests.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to Fremont, California, in an attempt to save a restaurant that is tearing the owners' marriage apart. Saki Kavouniaris took over Spin a Yarn in 1995 and began to turn it into a success, before marrying bartender Jennifer. When the business started to fail, the couple decided to splash out on refurbishments to recover their glory days, but their efforts have been futile and now their relationship is at stake.(n)

programme.title: A Day in the Life of Coronavirus
programme.desc: Documentary filmed on Friday 3 April, offering a look at lockdown in the UK over the course of 24 hours. It captures life for people at home and at work, revealing how those who are ill or long-term self-isolating are being cared for, and the daily activity of police, nurses, doctors, binmen and supermarket shelf-stackers.(n)

programme.title: The Undateables
programme.desc: Revisiting three familiar faces who are still looking for love, and one former singleton who is now engaged and is getting married to his college sweetheart. Charlotte, who has autism, is moving out of her family home and is looking for a life partner. Meanwhile, train-lover Richard from Warwickshire is still searching for someone to share his life with, as is Alex, who has written his own book about autism since last appearing on the programme. Finally the programme catches up with 26-year-old Sam, who is preparing to marry his old friend from college, Vanessa.(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the words-and numbers-quiz, inviting Sean Lock and Katherine Ryan to take on Jon Richardson and Joe Wilkinson. Dr John Cooper Clarke joins wordsmith Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while maths whizz Rachel Riley looks after the numbers.(n)

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: Carol Vorderman, Kelly Brook, Rob Rinder and Mo Gilligan take part in the final charity culinary challenge, overseen by judges Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith, and presenters Noel Fielding and Sandi Toksvig, making her final appearance in the tent before being replaced by Matt Lucas for the next series. The contestants must take on three tricky challenges - a sandwich biscuit for the signature round, a sweet bread-based technical, and a cake showstopper which reveals their favourite guilty pleasure.(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer guide prospective buyers in Yorkshire through the complex world of viewings and agents as they try to buy a home. Kirstie meets Leeds locals Mia and Jack, who are ready to move out of Mia's family home. Elsewhere, Phil is tasked to find a four bedroom family home for childhood sweethearts Rachel and Chris.(n)

programme.title: Animal Rescue School
programme.desc: The trainees must lead investigations for the first time, including cases of pigeons that have had their wings taped up and meerkats who need to be found a bigger home.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: A couple face a challenging build in the beach side suburb of North Bondi, Sydney, with no guarantee of how or when their multi-tiered house will ever be completed.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Karl and Naomh, who first met at university, love the relaxed lifestyle of Menorca and want to be near the beautiful beaches of its south-east coast where they held their wedding. With Scarlette Douglas on hand and a top cash budget of £230,000, they are looking for a place with plenty of outside space for themselves and their two dogs.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Belfast, ultra-competitive foodie Sarah hopes the combination of a completely home-made meal and unshakeable self-confidence will have her romping across the finishing line against ditzy boutique owner Fran, Brazilian-born fashion advisor Ricardo and dentist Ian.(n)

programme.title: Posh Pawnbrokers
programme.desc: Deals on the table today include a cross between a motorbike and a boat, rare vintage champagne, an unusual mechanical calculator and an Aboriginal weapon known as a nulla nulla.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.desc: The chef travels to Philadelphia to help failing Mexican restaurant Zocalo. Now Gordon tries to turn things around before the floundering business ruins their marriage and tears their family apart.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Cumbria is the location for three more couples to demonstrate their cooking and hosting skills, kicking off with teachers Rory and Sam, who serve up dishes inspired by Europe. Day two sees Sarka and Paul lay on a Czech menu, but the massive portions defeat everyone - except the ever-hungry Rory. Finally, it's the turn of John and Sally with their retro 1980s evening. Which couple will win the cash prize of £1,000?(n)

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra is upset at Ray's reaction to a friend's breast enlargement, and decides to play a trick on him to see if he really is happy with her body the way it is. Comedy, starring Ray Romano.(n)

programme.title: Dispatches
programme.sub-title: Coronavirus: How Britain Is Changing - Dispatches
programme.desc: As coronavirus takes hold of Britain, Dispatches investigates the long-term effects of the outbreak and asks how the deadly Covid-19 is changing Britain in the long term. As the death toll rises, the programme looks at everything from seismic changes in economic policy to shifts in the way we view and treat other people, and from a drop in pollution to the widening of the gaps between rich and poor, young and old. The programme asks what a post-Covid 19 Britain, torn apart by the disease, might look like, and the potential changes to the way people live their lives for years to come.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Paul and Ben face a challenging eviction in London, where a vulnerable tenant in rent arrears has barricaded her front door, and the agents receive a tirade of abuse. programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

Stewart and Vic track down a debt of £5,000 from the owner of a computer repair shop in Lincolnshire.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court enforcement agents Paul Bohill and Steve Pinner are in Birmingham on a potentially dangerous, full-scale eviction of a large traveller community. Meanwhile, in London's diamond district Brian O'Shaughnessy and Delroy Anglin are chasing nearly £6,000 owed by a jeweller to an unhappy customer, although a solicitor tries to convince Delroy his client owes nothing.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court Enforcement Agents Paul and Steve are sent to north London to evict a young Romanian mother who owes £6,000 in unpaid rent, but when they arrive, they discover a family home that is not quite what it appears to be. Meanwhile, in Kent, Brian and Alan turn up the pressure on a debtor who has admitted to owing more than £7,000, but claims he cannot afford to pay up - despite having a garage full of expensive tools. Later, in south-west London, Brian accompanies Delroy as he tries to recover a £400,000 debt from a residential property, and when the pair trace the defaulter to an upmarket cafe, the man in charge lies about his identity.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Stewart and Elmor attempt to collect £5,000 owed by a debtor in north Wales, but the situation spirals out of control when a group of friends try to take on the agents. Meanwhile, Del and Dael head to south-east London to chase nearly £7,000 owed by a man to his ex-girlfriend, before visiting a woman who owes her solicitor almost £4,000 after a compensation claim went wrong. In Manchester, Stewart and Vic face a hostile reception at a family run garage that owes £6,000 in professional fees.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In south London, Del and Dael Anglin receive a hostile reception from the brother of a debtor who has alreasy defaulted on a promise to pay £2,000 she owes to a storage company. In Worcestershire, Gareth and Craig try to recover £45,000 from a young online entrepreneur, who claims no responsibility for the debt he owes to his courier. Their target cooks up a flimsy lie in an effort to absolve himself, but dishonesty proves to be the least of the agents' worries when they discover a stash of guns at the back of the garage. In east London, an Indian restaurant owner begins to take his financial responsibilities seriously when Del and Dael start removing cookers and freezers from his premises, and a pair of pensioners in Oxfordshire hope the High Court will provide them a lifeline as they try to pay off a £4,000 bill to a solicitors' firm after a failed compensation claim.(n)

programme.title: Benefits Britain: Life on the Dole
programme.desc: This edition focuses on young people living on state welfare, including 18-year-old Dean from Harlow in Essex, who has no intention of getting a job unless forced. Twentysomethings Danielle and her boyfriend Josh are unable to work due to anxiety and depression and have moved to Blackpool to make a new start. However, with four children between them from previous relationships and another on the way they are struggling to make ends meet on a joint benefits income of around £100 a week.(n)

programme.title: House
programme.desc: The doctor struggles to diagnose apparently healthy teenager Della when she collapses during a skateboarding exhibition. Her parents already have a terminally ill son and face an impossible decision about the fate of their two children when her body rejects a donor lung.(n)

programme.title: House
programme.desc: When a children's author has a seizure that prevents her from committing suicide, the team is challenged to evaluate her underlying medical conditions and psychological state. As the examination progresses, House becomes convinced the answers to her ailments are hidden in the pages of her most recent novel. Later, the sharp-witted medic and Cuddy go on a double date with Wilson and Sam. Guest starring Amy Irving (Alias).(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: On Benefits
programme.desc: The stories of four people who have spent a total of almost 90 years on benefits. They include Greater Manchester couple Irene and Steve, who between them have been claiming for 70 years. Irene suffers with agoraphobia, and until recently husband Steve received a care allowance to look after her, but after a recent assessment their money has been cut by £6,000. Plus, a 49-year-old woman who once worked in the benefits office, only for her arthritis to leave her relying on the system, and a 57-year-old Navy veteran who suffers post-traumatic stress disorder after his experiences in the Gulf War.(n)

programme.title: On Benefits
programme.desc: The documentary series focuses on welfare recipients from the Merseyside area, including New Brighton man Jimmy, who struggles to find work as his religious beliefs prevent him from working on a Sunday, and Birkenhead single mum Nicola, who has become an expert bargain hunter to help stretch her benefits. Wallasey man Joe has a number of serious health conditions and hasn't had a shower at home in three years. However, after three years on the waiting list, a move to a specially adapted bungalow is finally on the cards.(n)

programme.title: Babies 24/7: Maternity Ward
programme.desc: After the traumatic birth of her first-born son, 36-year-old Michelle is determined to have an easier time with her second, but scans reveal their baby could be bigger than average.(n)

programme.title: Extraordinary People
programme.sub-title: The Boys with No Skin: Extraordinary People
programme.desc: The stories of two people who have epidermolysis bullosa, a group of rare inherited skin disorders that cause the skin to become very fragile. Eleven-year-old Charlie had a bone-marrow transplant five years ago that radically improved his condition and is now hoping to take part in a gene-editing clinical trial. Meanwhile, 17-old Jonathan has so far shunned radical new methods to combat the disease, but travels 1,000 miles to meet Charlie and his doctor to see what this ground-breaking transplant treatment could do for him.(n)

programme.desc: The hospital is placed in lockdown when a newborn goes missing, prompting staff members to open up to their colleagues in a series of heart-to-hearts that sees Cameron reunited with Chase when she returns to Princeton-Plainsboro. Directed by Hugh Laurie.(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Stavros and Emily try to get their house back from squatters, while Margaret attempts to recover the £20,000 she is owed from a letting agent, and Peterborough council find 12 people crammed into a category-one fire hazard house. Documentary series in which cameras follow the victims and villains of the buy-to-let boom.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In this special episode, the High Court enforcement agents have to deal with a fraudulent tenant, an evasive debtor and a mother in denial. Steve and Ben are in west London to see a tenant who owes £14,000 in unpaid rent, but when they gain access to the property they discover the house has divided up like a hotel and is being sublet. In Sussex, Brian and Del chase a debt of over £10,000 owed to a van rental business for damaged vehicles, before heading to Kent to try to reclaim £6,000 of unpaid rent from a woman financially dependent on a new partner after a disastrous divorce.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In south London, father-and-son team Del and Dael Anglin face death threats from a debtor's father, after arriving at a property to collect a debt of over £3,500. Meanwhile, in east London, two agents are confronted with a tenant who claims to have had no prior warning that he is being evicted from his tiny bedsit. Del and Brian try to recover £6,000 owed by a London hotel to a catering supply company, and Paul and Steve must tackle a difficult eviction.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Gareth and Craig investigate a possible case of mistaken identity in Shropshire, then in south Wales find themselves embroiled in a bitter dispute between two dog owners over a vet's bill. Steve and Phil have to evict a distraught young family from a former high street bank in west London, before paying a call on an elderly tailor in north London.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.sub-title: Can't Pay? My Nightmare Ex Wife
programme.desc: High Court enforcement agents face a raft of excuses and stalling tactics as they try to recover £3,000 from a young debtor in Manchester, and a Kent businessman tries to protect his company from his ex-wife by claiming that it belongs to his sister. A Staffordshire optician who owes money to a supplier claims that the equipment he was sold was faulty, while enforcement agents in north London are forced to mediate between a tenant who claims she is being evicted for having children and a landlord who says she wanted to leave in the hope of getting a council house.(n)

programme.title: Benefits Britain: Life on the Dole
programme.desc: Life on the dole in Scotland, including aspiring pop stars Zedd and Saffire, who live in Portobello, Edinburgh and receive £17,000 a year in benefits. After a failed attempt to start her own business in Australia, Kit is back in her home town of Wishaw, North Lanarkshire, to sign on and stay with friends, while Stirling resident Kirsty is desperate for work and is convinced her weight and mild learning disability are stopping her from getting a job. The 30-year-old's stay-at-home parents Rosie and Desmond believe that discrimination is to blame.(n)

programme.title: House
programme.desc: A patient suffering from uncontrollable vomiting is admitted, and after making unexpected discoveries about her identity, the team has to delve into her medical history when her condition does not improve. Meanwhile, with Thirteen absent, Chase hires a new member of staff who is made to feel unwelcome, and House has to confront his reservations about his relationship with Cuddy following a visit from his massage therapist.(n)

programme.title: House
programme.desc: When a newborn baby experiences breathing problems and liver failure, the infant's mother is left facing a decision that could affect the child's health as well as her own. House challenges Foreman and Taub to hire a female doctor, and learns a few things about parenting when he looks after Cuddy's daughter. Guest starring Jennifer Grey (Dirty Dancing).(n)

programme.title: Make You Laugh Out Loud
programme.sub-title: Naughty Toddlers Make You Laugh Out Loud
programme.desc: Iain Lee narrates a compilation of amusing clips of toddlers appearing on the internet, while contributors Bob Mills, Ellie Taylor and Marek Larwood comment on what they see. Plus, a behind-the-scenes look at the supervirals that have taken the web by storm, such as toddler pals Ava and Leo, whose adorable canoodling racked up more than 120 million views, and Tiffany Sims the doughnut-pilfering three-year-old whose sassy backchat made her a YouTube star.(n)

programme.title: Babies 24/7: Maternity Ward
programme.desc: Second-time mum Catherine is induced at the hospital three weeks early, as she is suffering from a liver condition that makes her itch profusely.(n)

programme.title: 10 Years Younger in 10 Days
programme.desc: Cherry Healy and the experts help Sylvia, who wants to get rid of the scars left by skin cancer and reverse the effects of severe hair loss, and Graham, whose traumatic memories of his time in the Army has led to years of heavy smoking and neglect for his appearance.(n)

programme.title: Creed
programme.desc: The son of a heavyweight boxer who died in the ring seeks to follow in the footsteps of the father he never knew. He goes in search of his father's old rival-turned-best friend Rocky Balboa, who agrees to train the young fighter for a shot at the title - but the ageing champ is facing challenges of his own. Boxing drama, starring Michael B Jordan, with Sylvester Stallone reprising his role from the Rocky movies.(n)

Police drama, starring Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: An acclaimed travel writer is found murdered with underwear stuffed in his mouth, and the unassuming boyfriend of his landlady comes under suspicion. programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Developers Lucy Alexander and Martin Roberts explore properties in Northern Ireland and southern England, including a flat, a Victorian house with seven separate rental rooms and a 1960s terraced home. After they are sold at auction, Lucy and Martin speak to the new owners about their plans for the buildings.(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.sub-title: DIY SOS at Great Ormond Street Hospital
programme.desc: The DIY SOS team are out of their comfort zone this week, joining forces with two world-famous institutions - Great Ormond Street Hospital and the Chelsea Flower Show - for a special challenge. Gardener Chris Beardshaw has promised his gold medal-winning garden from the flower show to the hospital - now all Nick Knowles and his fellow builders have to do is crane the display across London and rebuild it on the hospital. It's a hugely ambitious build, with tricky logistics and emotional volunteers. But knowing how much the garden will prove welcome respite for families, the team is determined not to fail.(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.sub-title: Million Pound Build for Children in Need
programme.desc: DIY SOS highlights the plight of nation's 250,000 young carers, who look after their parents on a daily basis. Blackpool Carers Trust, which is supported by funding from Children in Need, provides limited out-of-school respite and training activities for some of these carers - but its current centre is bursting at the seams. It has now inherited a run-down property, which is bigger but will require the renovation of a neglected building and a huge overgrown garden - and that's where Nick and the team come in, with help from designer Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen.(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles, his team and a group of volunteers are in Monmouth, Wales, to transform the family home of Charlotte, a mother recovering from a stroke that left her with brain damage, speech loss and partial paralysis. The current layout of her home makes it difficult for Charlotte to move around and have her physiotherapy treatment, so the team redesign the house to make things easier for her, her husband Rob and their children.(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to the Isle of Sheppey in Kent to meet two extraordinary dads, Garry and Kyle, who have adopted four vulnerable children - three of whom have special needs - to give them the best life possible. But their cramped three-bedroom bungalow is making family life difficult. So to give this family the space they so desperately need, the DIY SOS builders are undertaking their most ambitious project yet - to demolish the bungalow and build a bespoke five-bedroom house in its place. But with just nine days to complete the task, it looks like being their toughest one yet.(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles meets midwife and kidney patient Sascha, who has to undergo intense dialysis sessions for hours on end, five days a week. This places the mother-of-four's body under great stress and the constant threat of blood clots gives her a life expectancy of just a few years. Determined to help, the DIY SOS team, bolstered by the local tradespeople and volunteers of Welwyn Garden City, renovate her home to include a dedicated dialysis room, offering her the chance of many more years with her family.(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team return to Veterans Street in Manchester, where, two years ago, with the help of Prince William and Prince Harry, DIY SOS launched their most ambitious project yet - transforming a derelict street into a vibrant community for veterans and local people. This time they build the final home for decorated former soldier and single dad Simon Flores, whose foot was blown off by a roadside bomb during a patrol in Iraq.(n)

programme.title: Jamie's School Dinners
programme.desc: Eighteen months after his TV series which prompted Tony Blair to set new standards for school dinners, the celebrity chef returns to see if his attempts to get children eating healthier meals in school have actually had a lasting effect. After meeting dinner ladies, youngsters and parents, he comes to the conclusion that there is still work to be done, and heads to Downing Street with a new plan.(n)

programme.title: Brief Encounter
programme.desc: A suburban housewife meets a married doctor by chance in a railway station waiting room. Later, their paths cross many times until the pair become friends and their meetings a fixed arrangement. But as their feelings for each other transform from friendship into passion, both come to realise the affair is doomed to fail. David Lean's romantic drama, with Celia Johnson, Trevor Howard and Cyril Raymond.(n)

programme.title: The Case of the Frightened Lady
programme.desc: A woman employed as a secretary to her aristocratic aunt has to contend with sinister servants plotting behind her back and a scheme by her employer to marry her off to her own cousin. The situation is complicated further when the family's doctor is accused of murder. Thriller, starring Marius Goring and Penelope Dudley-Ward.(n)

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing lesser known areas of London for locals and tourists. Featuring Lee, Bermondsey and Stratford. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay.(n)

programme.title: Coach Trip
programme.desc: The tourists visit Cordoba, heading to a farm for some olive picking then taking a traditional Spanish folk-singing class, before they face a tense vote.(n)

programme.title: Coach Trip
programme.desc: In Malaga, bad behaviour on the golf course leaves Brendan red-faced, and there are some truly remarkable results when the tourists paint images of one another at a Picasso art class. It is the last chance for a red card at the vote, so the competitors will each be hoping to make it through to the final day.(n)

programme.title: Clipper: Race of Their Lives
programme.desc: Behind the scenes on the record-breaking 40,000 nautical mile race around the world on 70-foot ocean racing yachts. Follow the inside story of what they go through to take part.(n)

programme.title: Earth Cycle
programme.desc: Simon Parker charts the seasonal cycles of the natural world as he journeys through Scandinavia on a bicycle, wild camping, seeing animal migrations and sampling local food.(n)

programme.title: Earth Cycle
programme.desc: Simon Parker charts the seasonal cycles of the natural world as he journeys through Scandinavia on a bicycle, wild camping, seeing animal migrations and sampling local food.(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Exploring what it actually means to be transgender, and what happens when a person transitions psychologically, physically and biologically. Cameras follow a number of transgender people going through their own transition, including a socially transitioning transwoman and two young transmen embarking on hormones.(n)

programme.title: The Prosecution of Julian Assange
programme.desc: Chronicling the events that led to the prosecution of one of the most influential figures to emerge this century. Investigating this polarising figures who is lauded and dismissed as hero and villain.(n)

programme.title: Abraham's Point
programme.desc: A man who left his childhood home in Wales 15 years previously decides to face up to his troubled past. He steals a grandfather clock and takes it on a journey across the country, intending to return the vintage timepiece to his father, who is dying. Drama, starring Mackenzie Crook and Joseph Milson.(n)

programme.title: Meet the Trumps: From Immigrant to President
programme.desc: Donald Trump is the 45th President of the United States and commander in chief of America's armed forces, the most powerful position in the world. This documentary goes back to his roots, to reveal the story of his family. It reveals how Trump's penniless German grandfather arrived in America in the 1880s, and ended up running bars and brothels in the gold rush. And how his Scottish mother travelled from the Outer Hebrides to New York and worked as a maid before marrying Donald's tycoon father.(n)

programme.title: 198 Grand
programme.desc: Three hapless criminals from South London get mixed up in an underworld double-cross, and find themselves in possession of a large sum of money that puts their lives in danger. Crime drama, starring Andy Frith and Dean Kilbey.(n)

programme.desc: Exploring what it actually means to be transgender, and what happens when a person transitions psychologically, physically and biologically. programme.title: K3
programme.desc: Dr Frank Enstine erases Kylie and Kim's memories, and Kate has to remind them who they are in time for a concert.(n)

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: The band suffers a run of mishaps while in Acapulco, and discovers that a shaman has accidentally cursed them with bad luck.(n)

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Sven and Halvar work together to escape from a giant and steal his treasure - but the pirate captain tries to take all the loot for himself.(n)

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: The crew explores a tunnel full of traps - and Ulme's musical talents may be the key to everyone getting out in one piece.(n)

programme.desc: Jack and Tashi need a genie's help to defeat a monster, but find that he has been kicked out of his lamp.(n)

programme.title: The Last Days of Anne Boleyn
programme.desc: On Friday, May 19, 1536, Henry VIII's second wife Anne Boleyn became the first British queen to be executed, but the circumstances of her death remain shrouded in mystery and contradiction. Hilary Mantel, Philippa Gregory and David Starkey are among the leading authors and historians sifting through the evidence to determine why Henry had her killed.(n)

programme.title: The Importance of Being Earnest
programme.desc: Two bachelors using the same pseudonym capture the hearts of a couple of girls during a delightful weekend in the countryside. However, their cunning deception leads to all manner of hilarious confusion as the ladies in question attempt to work out who is who. Adaptation of Oscar Wilde's comedy, starring Rupert Everett, Colin Firth, Frances O'Connor, Reese Witherspoon, Judi Dench and Tom Wilkinson.(n)

programme.title: The Harassed Hero
programme.desc: A hypochondriac is ordered by his doctor to avoid stressful situations. Unfortunately, he inadvertently becomes involved in an explosive international criminal caper. Comedy, starring Guy Middleton and Joan Winmill Brown.(n)

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing Crystal Palace and Shoreditch. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: A property expert offers advice to British householders aiming to buy a home abroad.(n)

programme.title: Coach Trip
programme.desc: The tourists end their trip in Marbella in the Andalusia region of Spain. They race dune buggies and celebrate by creating some unique beverages at a cocktail-making class, before a couple are crowned the overall winners at voting time.(n)

programme.title: Coach Trip
programme.desc: As the remaining tourists prepare to make the long journey home, Brendan looks back on his favourite and funniest moments of their hugely eventful six-week adventure.(n)

programme.title: Diana: Last Days of a Princess
programme.desc: Genevieve O'Reilly and Patrick Baladi star as Diana, Princess of Wales and Dodi Al Fayed in a docu-drama charting the couple's last days, culminating in the fatal crash in the Alma Tunnel, Paris. Including interviews with Mohamed Al Fayed, bodyguard Kez Wingfield and masseuse Myriah Daniels.(n)

programme.title: Murder Investigation Team
programme.desc: When a convicted paedophile is discovered murdered and mutilated, the team find it difficult to see him as a victim and are in no rush to track down the culprit, so it falls to DI Friend to remind them they still have a job to do. The problem is, all his police files and personal records have gone missing - and the children he supposedly abused do not exist.(n)

programme.title: The Pink House
programme.desc: Documentary about a brothel in Australia's gold rush region that has been running for decades under the watchful eye of its now 70-year-old madam.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: Strike It Lucky
programme.desc: Michael Barrymore hosts the madcap game show, in which three teams of two prove seek to progress along a board game-style path by answering questions. Each duo are allowed to determine how many spaces they would like to move by answering the corresponding number of questions. Landing on a space reveals it's hidden contents, which could amount to a prize, or a 'hot spot', which causes contestants to forfeit their gathered winnings.(n)
